Metadata.title: Parabellum | Military Equipment Analytics
Metadata.description: Open-source military equipment loss tracking and analytics platform with verified data, interactive charts, and geospatial mapping.

Navigation.menuDescription: Quick access to the main sections of the platform.

HomePage.heroDescription: Verified OSINT data on military equipment losses across active conflicts. Photographic evidence, geolocation, and deep analytics — all in one platform.
HomePage.recentHint: Latest confirmed equipment losses with photographic evidence

Features.sectionDescription: Working tools for browsing loss records, filtering datasets, and exploring conflict data

liveTracking.title: Recent Loss Records
liveTracking.description: The homepage highlights the latest confirmed entries and links straight to detailed loss records with status, region, date, and map context.

interactiveMaps.title: Interactive Conflict Maps
interactiveMaps.description: A full-screen map combines equipment losses and conflict events, with filters by conflict, status, equipment type, and country.

deepAnalytics.title: Deep Analytics
deepAnalytics.description: Analytics pages surface totals, status breakdowns, category charts, cumulative views, and conflict-specific timelines.

advancedSearch.title: Advanced Search & Filters
advancedSearch.description: The losses table can be narrowed by status, equipment type, model, inscription, region, conflict, and date range.

verifiedData.title: Structured Loss Records
verifiedData.description: Each record is stored with normalized fields such as equipment, country, conflict, status, source record ID, and coordinates when available.

categoryBreakdown.title: Category Breakdown
categoryBreakdown.description: Equipment types are grouped into visual tiles and charts, making it easier to compare categories and spot dominant loss patterns.

Platform.sectionDescription: The main parts of the product that are already available in the current build

lossPages.title: Loss Record Pages
lossPages.description: Open a separate page for each entry with equipment details, status, date, region, source record number, and a direct jump to the map.

searchFilters.title: Search & Filters
searchFilters.description: Filter the analytics table by status, type, model, inscription, region, conflict, and date to narrow large datasets quickly.

analyticsBlog.title: Analytics Blog
analyticsBlog.description: The blog already supports published posts, category filters, read-time filters, and dedicated article pages.

conflictMaps.title: Conflict Maps
conflictMaps.description: The map view shows equipment losses and events on separate layers, with a conflict switcher and sidebar filters.

dashboards.title: Analytics Dashboard
dashboards.description: The analytics section combines summary totals, equipment-type tiles, a paginated losses table, and chart drill-downs.

trends.title: Trend Analysis
trends.description: Timeline, cumulative, category, and status charts help compare how losses change across conflicts and over time.

Blog.sectionDescription: In-depth research and analytical reports from our team

post1.title: Armored Vehicle Attrition Patterns in Modern Conflicts
post1.excerpt: A deep dive into how modern anti-tank systems have shifted the balance of armored warfare, with data from 14,000+ verified losses.

post2.title: OSINT Verification Methods: How We Confirm Equipment Losses
post2.excerpt: Our methodology for cross-referencing satellite imagery, social media posts, and official reports to build a reliable dataset.

post3.title: The Rise of UAV Losses: A 2024-2026 Trend Analysis
post3.excerpt: Drone warfare has transformed the battlefield. This analysis tracks the exponential growth in UAV attrition across multiple theaters.

Charts.conflictHint: Switch the selected armed conflict to update the charts below.

AboutPage.title: What is Parabellum?
AboutPage.description: Parabellum is an independent analytics platform focused on armed conflicts and the documented losses of military equipment used within them.
AboutPage.missionBody: Our goal is to give researchers, journalists, and the public a clear, verifiable, and as objective a view as possible of equipment losses across 21st-century conflicts. We combine OSINT workflows, structured datasets, visual analytics, and modern verification tools to do that well.
AboutPage.policyTitle: Do you track Russian military losses?
AboutPage.policyBody: No, <bold>Parabellum will never track Russian losses</bold>, regardless of the conflict, theater, or countries involved.
AboutPage.donationsBody: No. If you want to support people who genuinely need help, please learn more through our friends here: <link>recommended fundraiser</link>.

BlogPage.title: Blog
BlogPage.description: In-depth analytical reports, OSINT methodologies, and conflict trend research from our team.
BlogPage.noPostsHint: Try adjusting your filters or check back later.

AnalyticsPage.title: Analytics
AnalyticsPage.description: Equipment loss statistics across armed conflicts

NotFoundPage.title: Sorry, you are here by mistake.
NotFoundPage.description: This page is either under development, or an unknown error happened. We know about that and are already investigating the issue.
